description: Use this API to calculate and replicates the state variable system matrices in the form required by the Linear Dynamic Analysis Program, LSYSAN (activity ASTR). This API routine was first introduced in release 30.2.0. ierr = astr(option, maxderiv, perturb, nstate, minstate, maxstate, noutput, minchan, maxchan, ninput, intype, inpert, inbus, inmcid, inident, outfile)
python_syntax: ierr = astr(option, maxderiv, perturb, nstate, minstate, maxstate, noutput, minchan, maxchan, ninput, intype, inpert, inbus, inmcid, inident, outfile)

parameters.name: option
parameters.description: Network solution convergence monitor option. (input; 0 by default).

parameters.name: maxderiv
parameters.description: Largest derivative threshold for considering that the initialization reflects a steady-state condition. (input; 0.01 by default).

parameters.name: perturb
parameters.description: Amount by which the specified state variables are to be perturbed. (input; 0.01 by default).

parameters.name: nstate
parameters.description: Number of state variable ranges defining those state variables that are to be perturbed; 0 to include all states. (input; 0 by default).

parameters.name: minstate
parameters.description: An array of NSTATE elements specifying a list of starting STATE indices defining the ranges of state variables to be perturbed; ignored if NSTATE = 0. (input; no default allowed).

parameters.name: maxstate
parameters.description: An array of NSTATE elements specifying a list of ending STATE indices defining the ranges of state variables to be perturbed; ignored if NSTATE = 0. (input; no default allowed).

parameters.name: noutput
parameters.description: Number of channel ranges defining those output channels that are to be included in the system output vector; 0 to use the first ‘nchan’ or 50, whichever is less. (input; 0 by default).

parameters.name: minchan
parameters.description: An array of NOUTPUT elements specifying a list of starting channel indices defining the ranges of output channels to be included in the system output vector; ignored if NOUTPUT = 0. (input; no default allowed).

parameters.name: maxchan
parameters.description: An array of NOUTPUT elements specifying a list of ending channel indices defining the ranges of output channels to be included in the system output vector; ignored if NOUTPUT = 0. (input; no default allowed).

parameters.name: ninput
parameters.description: Number of system variables that are to be perturbed. (input; 0 by default).

parameters.name: intype
parameters.description: An array of NINPUT elements specifying a list of variable type codes; ignored if NINPUT = 0. (input; no default allowed).

parameters.name: inpert
parameters.description: An array of NINPUT elements specifying a list of perturbation factors to be applied to those system variables that are to be perturbed; ignored if NINPUT = 0. (input; PERTURB by default).

parameters.name: inbus
parameters.description: An array of NINPUT elements specifying a list of buses (when the corresponding INTYPE is 1 through 4) and/or VAR indices (when the corresponding INTYPE is 5) used to define those system variables that are to be perturbed; ignored if NINPUT = 0. (input; no default allowed).

parameters.name: inmcid
parameters.description: An array of NINPUT elements specifying a list of machine identifiers used to define those system variables that are to be perturbed (when the corresponding INTYPE is 1 through 4; ignored when the corresponding INTYPE is 5); ignored if NINPUT = 0. (input; ‘1’ by default).

parameters.name: inident
parameters.description: An array of NINPUT elements specifying a list of text strings used to identify those system variables that are to be perturbed; ignored if NINPUT = 0. If a blank identifier is specified, the ASTR API provides an appropriate identifier. (input; blank by default).

parameters.name: outfile
parameters.description: Name of the matrix output file into which the results of this api’s calculation are to be placed; this file may then be used as to the lsysan program. (input; no default allowed).

option.description: print the convergence monitor only if it is enabled via the CM interrupt control code.

intype.description: PMECH, turbine mechanical power (pu on MBASE).

error_codes.description: derivative threshold exceeded–not in steady state.
invalid code of input to be perturbed.
attempting to select more STATEs than are in the system model.
prior initialization modified the loads–pick up original converted case.
prerequisite requirements for API are not met.
